channel.title: AI Thought Leader Talks (YouTube)
channel.description: Original long-form talks and interviews with AI thought leaders, filtered by Claude.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Morgan Stanley TMT Conference 2026 | Jensen Huang on AI, Compute, Tokens and the New Global Economy
item.author: Morgan Stanley
item.description:
Morgan Stanley · 43 min

From the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference in San Francisco, Jensen Huang explains how AI is reshaping the global economy. Core insight: Compute creates tokens, tokens power intelligence, and intelligence translates into economic output — at every level, from companies to countries.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Jensen Huang: Nvidia's Future, Physical AI, Rise of the Agent, Inference Explosion, AI PR Crisis
item.author: All-In Podcast
item.description:
All-In Podcast · 66 min

Jensen Huang joins the All-In Podcast to discuss: acquiring Groq and the inference explosion, decision making at the world's most valuable company, Physical AI's $50T market, OpenClaw's future, the new operating system for modern AI computing, AI's PR crisis, refuting doomer narratives, Anthropic's comms mistakes, revenue capacity, token allocation for employees, Karpathy's autoresearch, agentic future, open source, global diffusion, Iran/Taiwan supply chain impact, self-driving platform, datacenters in space, AI healthcare, robotics, OpenAI/Anthropic revenue potential, how to build an AI moat, advice to young people on excelling in the AI era.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] NVIDIA CEO Jensen Huang GTC 2026 Full Keynote
item.author: Yahoo Finance
item.description:
Yahoo Finance · 140 min

Jensen Huang delivered his annual GTC keynote in San Jose, California. Key points: Nvidia's acqui-hire agreement with AI inference chip designer Groq, which may be integrated into Nvidia processors or offered as a new chip. Nvidia's strategic partner Nebius also reached a deal with Meta to provide up to $27 billion worth of capacity starting in 2027.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] Jensen Huang On NVIDIA Stock, OpenClaw, AI Demand - NVDA Update
item.author: FinVid
item.description:
FinVid · 37 min

Jensen Huang speaks with CNBC's Jim Cramer about NVIDIA stock, OpenClaw, autonomous vehicles, big tech, physical AI, NemoClaw, Vera Rubin, NVIDIA's future, and the broader AI landscape. Key disclosures: NVIDIA has received US and China approval to sell H200 chips in China; China purchase orders are coming in; supply chain is working to restart H200 manufacturing. NVIDIA product roadmap: Blackwell Ultra ramping, Rubin on track for 2026, Groq 3 LPX expected H2 2026, Rubin CPX end of 2026, Rubin Ultra in 2027, Feynman in 2028. Huang believes there will be $3-4T global AI factory buildout through 2030.

item.title: [Jensen Huang] LIVE: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang Unveils AI Innovations | DRM News | AI1F
item.author: DRM News
item.description:
DRM News · 189 min

Jensen Huang delivers the keynote address at Nvidia's AI conference in San Jose. The CEO unveiled the latest advancements in artificial intelligence, GPU technologies, and AI-driven innovations shaping the future of computing.

item.title: [Org: OpenAI Talks] Building AI for better healthcare — the OpenAI Podcast Ep. 14
item.author: OpenAI
item.description:
OpenAI · 31 min

OpenAI's Head of Health Dr. Nate Gross and Karan Singhal, who leads Health AI Research, discuss how AI can help address the biggest challenges in healthcare systems worldwide. Topics include: how OpenAI is training models to handle sensitive health questions in collaboration with physicians, how that foundation is unlocking a new generation of tools for patients, clinicians, and healthcare systems. Chapters cover origins of their interest in AI and healthcare, strategy for building AI tools for clinicians, training methods for health use cases, scoring on health evals, key challenges deploying AI in healthcare, collaboration with hospitals, practical everyday uses of AI health assistants, and biggest wow moments during development.

item.title: [Org: Meta AI Talks] Meta NeuroScientist SHOCKED Me: Scale Alone Won’t Create Consciousness!
item.author: Dr Brian Keating
item.description:
Dr Brian Keating · 51 min

David Sussillo (Meta Reality Labs, Stanford) explains why scaling transformers alone won't produce conscious AI and why studying the brain may be the only way forward. He discusses how recurrent neural networks differ from transformer architectures, the FORCE algorithm for training RNNs, Meta's EMG wristband research, Rich Sutton's Bitter Lesson, and why the GPU-transformer paradigm may have hit a ceiling. Sussillo spent years training neural networks at Google Brain and now leads research at Meta Reality Labs. He is also the author of Emergence, a braided memoir about his path from foster care to the frontier of neuroscience and AI research.

item.title: [Channel: Y Combinator] How AI Is Unlocking Millions Of New Builders
item.author: Y Combinator
item.description:
Y Combinator · 40 min

Mukund and Madhav Jha, founders of Emergent, discuss their AI platform that lets anyone build and ship production-ready software. In just eight months, users have created more than 7 million apps on Emergent, with the number doubling in the last 45 days. They cover how they built one of the most powerful AI coding agents, why they focused on non-technical users, what it's like building for a global audience from India, getting ahead of the market, the pivot to non-technical users, why second movers can win in AI, building for production rather than prototypes, how Emergent hires and runs a lean team, whether SaaS is dead with the rise of personalized software, and the future of niche apps, solo builders and AI agency.

item.title: [Channel: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast] A 7-hour marathon interview with Saining Xie: World Models, AMI Labs, Yann LeCun, Fei-Fei Li, and 42
item.author: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast
item.description:
Zhang Xiaojun Podcast · 405 min

Saining Xie's first-ever in-depth interview, a 7-hour marathon conversation. Xie, co-founder and Chief Science Officer of AMI Labs alongside Turing Award laureate Yann LeCun, discusses world models. AMI Labs has 25 people, zero products, yet raised a $1.03 billion Seed round at a $3.5 billion pre-money valuation — the largest Seed round in European history. Topics include: Xie's path from Shanghai Jiao Tong University to NYU faculty, his years at Google DeepMind and Meta FAIR, why Silicon Valley is 'hypnotized' by LLMs, his contrarian views on Scaling Laws ('there is significant water in language model scaling'), why language may be 'poison' for vision, his two refusals of Ilya Sutskever, memories of Yann LeCun and Fei-Fei Li, the genesis of AMI Labs, what a world model actually is, and the technological upheavals of the past 15 years. The interview was conducted in February 2026 in Brooklyn during a harsh New York winter.
